title: oriastrum lycopodioides
intro: Oriastrum lycopodioides, also known as the Club Moss, is a type of ancient vascular plant that was once abundant on Earth but is now extinct. Historically, the plant's spores were used in traditional medicine, particularly for their potential anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which made them useful for treating wounds and skin conditions. The plant's medicinal properties are believed to have numerous health benefits, including reducing inflammation, soothing pain, and promoting wound healing, although these claims are largely based on historical records. In traditional herbal preparations, the spores of Oriastrum lycopodioides were often dried and powdered to be used in topical creams and ointments.
